news.title: WSB invests ₹75 crore in Omaxe Group's projects; Karyan Group to invest ₹900 crore on Ghaziabad project
news.para: WSB Partners has injected ₹75 crore into Omaxe Group's residential plotted projects in Ujjain and Indore for growth capital. Meanwhile, Karyan Group announced a ₹900 crore investment for its Ghaziabad residential project, Trevana Residences, anticipating ₹1,500 crore in revenue from 608 units.

news.title: Green nod delay holds up Thiruvanthapuram airport expansion
news.para: Expansion of Thiruvananthapuram International Airport's Terminal 2 is delayed. Environmental clearance is pending with state authorities. The process involves multiple scrutiny stages. This delay impacts the planned April 2025 construction start. The airport operator needs this expansion for future passenger growth. Redevelopment includes terminal modernization, parking, and new facilities.

news.title: Rajkot civic body collects ₹152 crore advance taxes in 40 days
news.para: Rajkot Municipal Corporation has collected Rs 153 crore in advance property and water taxes for the 2026-27 financial year within 40 days. Digital payments led the collection, with significant discounts offered to early payers. This advance collection shows a steady increase from the previous year across all city zones.

news.title: Orissa high court stays suspension on sand quarry lessee
news.para: The Orissa High Court has ruled that statutory authorities cannot usurp powers not expressly conferred by law. The court stayed an interim suspension order on a sand quarry lessee, finding no provision in the Odisha Minor Mineral Concession Rules, 2016, for such immediate action pending adjudication of alleged violations.

news.title: Rajasthan: RIICO gets nearly 800 applications for 465 plots
news.para: RIICO received nearly 800 applications for 465 industrial plots valued at Rs 550 crore, primarily for the Kankani Industrial Area in Jodhpur. To boost occupancy in underutilised zones, RIICO revised its policy, offering plots at 60% of the prevailing rate. This initiative saw a significant rise in applications for these areas.

news.title: NeoLiv acquires 76 acres land in Maharashtra's Khopoli for plotted development, villa project
news.para: NeoLiv has acquired a 76-acre land parcel in Khopoli near Mumbai. This marks their third plotted development in the Panvel-Khopoli belt. The company plans a mixed-use community with villas, plots, and retail. The project has an estimated Gross Development Value of Rs 800 crore. This acquisition is a significant milestone for NeoLiv's expansion in a sought-after micro-market.

news.title: Mohali development body officials submit records to ED in ₹150 crore CLU fraud probe
news.para: Two senior Greater Mohali Area Development Authority (Gmada) officials appeared before the Enforcement Directorate (ED) in Jalandhar regarding a Rs 150 crore money laundering probe. The ED is investigating the approval processes for major real estate projects, examining potential bypasses of regulatory norms. This follows raids on several entities involved in these projects.

news.title: Uttar Pradesh cabinet approves land transfer for Agra metro corridor expansion
news.para: The Uttar Pradesh Cabinet has approved the transfer of land for Corridor-II of the Agra Metro Rail Project, specifically for the Agra Cantt-Kalindi Vihar metro corridor. This move aims to reduce traffic congestion, save travel time, and enhance urban infrastructure for Agra's residents and tourists.

news.title: Haryana cabinet approves revised project cost of Gurugram metro project
news.para: Haryana Cabinet approved a revised cost of over Rs 10,000 crore for the Gurugram Metro project, connecting Millennium City Centre to Cyber City. The decision also includes integration with Rapid Metro, a spur to Gurugram Railway Station, and funding the soft loan portion through the World Bank.

news.title: Puravankara posts net profit of ₹56.75 crore in FY26
news.para: Puravankara reported a net consolidated profit of ₹56.75 crore for FY26, a significant turnaround from a loss of ₹182.92 crore in FY25. The company's total income surged by 83.76% to ₹3,846.42 crore. In Q4 FY26, profit after tax reached ₹109.95 crore, with pre-sales growing by 190% to ₹3,547 crore.

news.title: China's home price falls narrow, but recovery may be months away
news.para: China's new home prices saw their slowest monthly decline in a year in April, indicating tentative stabilization. However, a broader market recovery remains uneven, with major cities showing firmer prices while smaller ones struggle with oversupply. Analysts suggest the property market may take years to fully bottom out.

news.title: Evergrande liquidators seek $8.4 billion from PwC, accusing it of negligent audits
news.para: Evergrande's liquidators are suing PwC for 57 billion yuan ($8.4 billion) in damages, alleging negligence in its auditing of the collapsed Chinese property giant. This claim follows significant fines and reprimands from Chinese and Hong Kong authorities against PwC for its role in the developer's downfall, which left over $300 billion in liabilities.

news.title: Gujarat aims to strengthen services sector, plans policy to attract GCCs
news.para: Gujarat is looking to expand its presence in emerging sectors such as artificial intelligence, semiconductors, global capability centres and advanced manufacturing, state officials said at an event in Ahmedabad. Union Home Minister Amit Shah said tech-led infrastructure will be important for Ahmedabad if the city has to compete with markets such as Bengaluru, Hyderabad, Pune and Gurugram.

news.title: Table Space adds five lakh sq ft workspace in Hyderabad; TEC expands 4.65 lakh sq ft in South India
news.para: Flexible workspace provider Table Space has significantly expanded its Hyderabad presence, adding five lakh sq ft of managed office space across key business districts. Meanwhile, The Executive Centre is bolstering its South India footprint with substantial new centers in Bengaluru, Hyderabad, and Chennai, collectively adding over 4.65 lakh sq ft and 6,000 workstations.

news.title: Ganesh Housing plans ₹15,000 crore investment in Ahmedabad project; signs ₹500 crore LRD
news.para: Ganesh Housing is developing Million Minds Tech City in Ahmedabad with a total investment plan of about ₹15,000 crore, including land value and construction cost. The company has completed the first phase of 13.5 lakh sq ft commercial space and has leased about 8 lakh sq ft so far. It has also signed a loan against rental discounting of about ₹500 crore, which will be used for further development.

news.title: Cement capex delayed due to execution challenges; focus on completing ongoing projects: Karan Adani
news.para: Ambuja Cements acknowledges project execution delays due to contractor issues and incomplete engineering, leading to a moderate FY27 capex of Rs 6,000-6,500 crore. The company is prioritizing project completion over new ventures, focusing on organic growth and greenfield expansion while evaluating inorganic opportunities.

news.title: Vijayawada civic body to introduce QR-based door numbers for properties
news.para: Vijayawada Municipal Corporation is introducing a unique digital identity for every property.  QR codes will replace existing door numbers for 2.40 lakh households. This initiative aims to speed up civic services, improve transparency in tax assessments, and streamline municipal administration.  Citizens will access property information digitally. The AP urban digital registry exercise will begin soon across the city.

news.title: Mumbai-based builder held for illegally selling SRA project flats
news.para: Mumbai police's Economic Offences Wing has arrested builder Sohail Abdullah Dukka for allegedly selling flats in a Jogeshwari slum redevelopment project illegally. Dukka, already in jail, is accused of misappropriating crores by selling flats independently and diverting payments into his personal accounts, bypassing designated RERA-linked accounts.
